hooks.command: bash -c 'jq -r .tool_input.file_path 2>/dev/null | while read f; do case "$f" in *.py) (cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && ruff check --fix --quiet "$f" 2>/dev/null; black --quiet "$f" 2>/dev/null) ;; *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx|*.json) (cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && prettier --write --log-level silent "$f" 2>/dev/null) ;; esac; done; exit 0'
cat >/dev/null || true; git rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1 && code-review-graph update --skip-flows --repo "/Users/aditya/Documents/Mission/voice-ai-receptionist" || true
cat >/dev/null || true; git rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1 && code-review-graph status --repo "/Users/aditya/Documents/Mission/voice-ai-receptionist" || echo 'Not a git repo, skipping'

_comment: Hooks auto-format Python files with ruff+black and JS/TS files with prettier after every Edit/Write. Errors are swallowed so a missing formatter never blocks an edit. To disable a hook temporarily, comment it out and restart Claude Code.
